description:
Metapack Engineering S.r.l is an Italian company which operates in the field of industrial automation since 2000.

Mainly specialized in the fields of pharmaceuticals, chemicals, food and beverage, and converting, Metapack provides for each phase of the solution deployment, from Customer URS analisys, through Detailed Design and machine manufacturing, up to the FAT/SAT processes and final installation, training and take-over support.

Among the specific competences of Metapack Engineering:
•	Late stage customization of cartons, labels, blister and Pharma Foils
•	Digital Identification/Printing systems (DOD UV, TIJ, CIJ and LASER printing)
•	Vision systems (COGNEX certified integrator) 
•	Traceability systems (pharmaceutical serialization, aggregation and related technologies)
•	Fast Decoding and print quality inspection in one system (V-Plus)
•	Blow Fill Seal Monodoses - Print and fill level inspection
•	Blister Single - Print and inspection
•	Print on Pharma Foils

Over the years, Metapack has developed uninterrupted tracking systems not only in Italy.
Many of our customers are pharmaceutical companies from all over the world (American, Asian and European companies). Our assistance services, guaranteed on site for the whole time of the cooperation, are structured with a team of engineers all over the world.

The entire production chain is carried out within the industrial plant. The design is managed by our team of engineers, who allows us to create machines that can be customized for all needs.

We are looking for new projects, 
Keep in touch

Eekels achieves a turnover of approximately 100 million euros. In the Netherlands, Eekels is located in Kolham (head office), Zaandam, Emmen, Gorinchem, Delfzijl and Joure. name: PSI Technics
description:
Our vision is simple:
We want to improve workflows to enhance your profitability.

The digital transformation is well underway. We help you to meet challenges in production and logistics while sustainably improving quality and throughput.

Karl-Heinz Förderer founded PSI Technics in 2005 in Koblenz, Germany. His passion for innovative, future-oriented and custom solutions is the driving force behind new developments and continuously improved processes that extend to a broad range of industrial sectors. We assist customers with optimizing and digitalizing workflows in production, railway transport and logistics, leveraging the multidisciplinary know-how of our engineers and the creativity of our developers.

We design our solutions as part of a custom, comprehensive approach aimed at increasing throughput and quality, saving resources and minimizing claims and rejects. PSI Technics offers reliable solutions for companies across all industries that provide added value for its customers. Market-leading companies around the world rely on PSI Technics as a full-service engineering provider.

We focus on:
Industrial image processing, advanced industrial positioning systems for ASRS/crane systems, industrial automation solutions, automated train inspection and rolling stock maintenance / railway maintenance, data mining, artificial intelligence and thermal protection/cooling cases for sensitive sensors. Our portfolio also includes modernization and retrofitting solutions as well as analyses for enhanced throughput and system availability. Due to our extensive expertise we are well equipped for taking on projects in a wide variety of fields. We invite you to put us to the test!

AUTOMATE. OPTIMIZE. INCREASE QUALITY. SAVE ENERGY.

We are the PGA Gesellschaft für Prozess- und Gebäudeautomatisierungstechnik mbH. For more than 20 years we have been working according to the future-oriented and worldwide standards for continuous integration and automation. Our goal is to develop flexible and timely solutions in process and building automation, which can be created according to customer-specific requirements. These solutions are environmentally friendly, energy and resource-saving, thus striving for the goals of Industry 4.0.

We plan, develop and implement integrated system solutions across the entire spectrum of modern digital automation technology. As a system integrator and solution provider, we provide completely integrated solutions for all applications of the:  

- Process and industrial automation
- Building technology
- Room and building automation
- Facility Management
- Environmental, water and sewage technology
- Energy management, energy and process data processing
- WEB-based HMI, SCADA and management solutions

name: Sweeper
description: SWEEPER’s main objective is to put the first generation greenhouse harvesting robots onto the market. Until now this has never been achieved and it will ensure Europe’s leading role in agricultural robotics.  In modern greenhouses there is a high demand to automate labour. The availability of a skilled workforce that accepts repetitive tasks in the harsh climate conditions of a greenhouse is decreasing rapidly. The current state of the art in automated harvesting of fruits and vegetables has remained remarkably stationary in the past decades. In the EU-FP7-project CROPS (www.crops-robots.eu) extensive research has been performed on agricultural robotics. One of the applications was a sweet pepper harvesting robot. SWEEPER will use the technology developed in CROPS to introduce, test and validate a robotic harvesting solution for sweet pepper under real-world conditions.  The project involves 6 partners from 4 different countries (The Netherlands, Belgium, Sweden and Israel). The consortium consist out of fundamental and applied research organisations, a system integrator and a modern grower of sweet pepper. In the consortium a wide-range of disciplines are available, including: horticulture, horticultural engineering, machine vision, sensing, robotics, control, intelligent systems, software architecture, system integration and greenhouse crop management.

name: Robotec Solutions
description: Robotec Solutions designs and implements customized robot-based automation solutions for industrial customers. The company develops individual robot cells, testing stations, and fully integrated production lines that cover production and manufacturing, material flow and intralogistics, and quality assurance and testing. Its systems include mobile robots, cobots, industrial robots, AI-supported visual inspection, rails and traversing axes, and complete robot cells and lines. Robotec Solutions serves electronics and semiconductors, plastics, food, luxury goods and watches, medtech and pharma, and the metal industry. Its services span feasibility studies, concept and system design, project management, commissioning, maintenance, support, extensions, training, and retrofit solutions. The company also offers a practice-integrated study program in cooperation with a university of applied sciences.

name: ROVIMATICA

The company designs, manufactures and commissions robotic cells, assembly and manufacturing lines, automated inspection systems, autonomous vehicles and PLC/SCADA control systems. Its teams work across mechatronics, electrical engineering, industrial robotics, AGVs and AMRs, automation and control, and machine vision and artificial intelligence. ROVIMATICA also delivers turnkey projects, covering concept design, engineering, programming, installation, start-up and maintenance. In addition, it runs R&D and innovation projects in machine vision, AI, cognitive robotics, edge computing and software-defined vehicle platforms for multiple industrial sectors. These solutions are applied in areas such as logistics, automotive, agrifood, recycling, energy, mobility and infrastructure management.

We can assist with maintenance, commissioning installation and programming as well as designing, conceptualising and planning of projects. With skilled engineers based in Switzerland, Germany, Austria, Morocco, Egypt and the Czech Republic, we can quickly and efficiently provide engineering resources to anywhere in the World. Most recently we have worked on projects for clients in the United States, Germany, Poland and Mexico.

name: AUTFORCE Vision Systems
description: AUTFORCE Vision Systems adds three business areas to a portfolio that is capable of performance, with services for projects of any size. The company offers solutions in the fields of machine vision, industrial identification and laser marking. The management of AUTFORCE Vision Systems GmbH is in the responsibility of Mr. Mag. (FH) Stefan Perg and Mr. Markus Piffer. The young company, headquartered in Lienz, East Tyrol, specializes in automation mechatronics. In three interrelated but also detached functional business areas, the expert team realizes tailor-made solutions from A to Z - from the in-depth feasibility study to implementation for commissioning, quality assurance and traceability. Today, industrial image processing systems face the challenge of having to meet a multitude of objectives in a dynamic environment and increasingly complex contexts. From 100% quality assurance through clear traceability to internal process optimization and increased fail-safety, the expectations of the manufacturing industry are sufficient. Three business areas complement each other to form a performance-oriented portfolio of machine vision, Industrial Identification and Lasermarking by working together with the affiliated company AUTFORCE Automations GmbH, complete automation solutions are just as possible as individual projects or just the purchase of suitable hardware.

name: Silicann Systems
description: Silicann Systems develops and produces optoelectronic sensor solutions and optical inspection and measurement systems for industrial and laboratory automation. The company focuses on NIR spectrometers, including the Spektralwerk Core series for inline inspection, identification, and content analysis in process environments. It offers application software such as Spectrotron and APIs for configuring and operating its spectrometers. Silicann Systems provides engineering services covering custom optical sensor and light source design, spectrometric calibration models, and algorithm development. Its electronics services include circuit and PCB design, firmware and embedded Linux development, interface integration, and prototyping through to small-series production. These offerings target research organizations and manufacturing companies that require precise photometric, spectral, and color measurement solutions integrated into automated processes.
